# Navigation

<!-- Generated by @create-component-md. Source of truth: Figma → .md migration. -->
<!-- Figma: https://www.figma.com/design/mhUvPg9tOjlvQvEW6glQhJ/Wi-Design-System?node-id=110-5385 -->
<!-- Generated at: 2026-07-21T16:30:00Z -->
<!-- Optional context provided: none -->

## Overview

Navigation is a fixed, 4-tab bottom navigation bar (Home, Card, Services, Menu) with a circular center floating action ("scan") button splitting the tabs 2-and-2. Each tab is a hardcoded composition of a 24×24 Lucide icon and a 12px label; the whole component has a single variant axis that selects which tab is currently active (icon + label switch from a neutral gray to the primary content color). This is not a generic repeatable tab list — each of the 4 tabs is its own fixed layer with its own icon and label, not an interchangeable slot.

**Variant axes.** `activeTab`: home (default), card, services, menu — a single Figma variant axis ("Property 1") that recolors whichever tab's icon + label is currently selected.

**Composition.** Every top-level child the plugin walked (the wrapper frame, the four "Icon with Text" tab frames, and the "Scan Button Container") was classified `decorative` by the extraction — none of them are themselves component instances. One level deeper, however, each tab's icon **is** a real instance (Lucide icons: `house`, `credit-card`, `layout-grid`, `settings-2`); the plugin's top-level walk did not descend far enough to classify these. They are documented here as fixed, non-configurable icon references (see Structure) rather than as a formal constitutive/referenced classification, since the automated classification pass never reached them — see Known gaps.

## Known gaps

- **[high] Wrapper frame layer names are inconsistent across variants despite identical geometry.** The single top-level wrapper is named "Home Container" (activeTab=home), "Container" (activeTab=card), "Menu option" (activeTab=services), and "Menu Item" (activeTab=menu) — four different names for what measures as the exact same 343×96 frame with identical padding in every variant. This is an authoring inconsistency in the Figma file, not a real structural difference; do not key any logic off these wrapper names. Re-extraction won't fix this — it needs a design-file cleanup.
- **[high] Root top-border color does not consistently track the active tab.** Only `activeTab=home` measures a colored top border (`Border/Primary #dde8fb`); `activeTab=card/services/menu` all measure a plain gray border (`Background/Neutral #e5e5e5`) instead of a matching highlight. Measured fact — flag for design review before assuming the border is meant to be static.
- **[medium] The center "scan" button's icon identity is unresolved.** It's a raw multi-path `VECTOR` (5 stroke paths) inside a 32×32 frame, not a named component instance — the extraction cannot tell you what icon it is (likely a scan/QR icon based on shape context, but that's a guess, not evidence). Its accessible name is unresolved for the same reason (see Voice).
- **[medium] "Status Indicator" rectangle has no resolved fill or stroke.** A 41×8 `RECTANGLE` sits alongside the scan button's icon frame with zero paint captured in `colorWalk`. Purpose is unclear from the extraction — could be an inert layout marker left over from design work rather than shipped UI. Confirm with design before implementing it.
- **[medium] Scan button pill has no resolved background fill.** The 56×56 circular "div" that hosts the center icon shows `fill: None` in every variant — either it's intentionally transparent (icon floats over whatever sits behind it) or it uses a gradient/image the extraction doesn't capture. Verify with design.
- **[low] Voice/Screen-reader section is inference, not Figma-sourced fact.** `_base.json` carries no aria layer names or accessible-name overrides. Tab semantics (`role=tab`, `aria-selected`) follow standard bottom-navigation convention given the `activeTab` evidence; verify with an accessibility reviewer.
- The extraction itself logged one warning: *"Dropped 2 raw-hex colorWalk entries that lived inside crossed INSTANCE boundaries (Navigation: 2). Tokened entries were preserved."* — two untokened color reads inside the nested icon instances were discarded; the tokened equivalents shown in this document are intact.

## Follow-ups

- **Optional per-icon specs.** The four tab icons (`house`, `credit-card`, `layout-grid`, `settings-2`) and the center scan icon are Lucide/design-system primitives that likely already have (or deserve) their own canonical specs elsewhere in the icon library — not required for this `.md` to be complete.
- **Design-file cleanup candidate.** Normalizing the four inconsistent wrapper-frame names (Known gaps, item 1) and confirming the top-border / scan-pill-fill behavior (items 2, 5) would remove every ambiguity flagged above without needing a new extraction.

**Cross-section invariants.** Root dimensions (343×96) and padding are identical across all 4 `activeTab` values — only the Color section's tab-highlight tokens change. The scan button's geometry (56×68 outer / 56×56 pill / 32×32 icon) is likewise constant across every variant.

---

## API

### Main table

| Property | Values | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `activeTab` | home, card, services, menu | false | `home` | Selects which fixed tab is visually highlighted (icon + label switch from `Content/Neutral #a3a3a3` to `Content/Primary #0f172a`). Source: Figma "Property 1" variant axis. |

**General notes.** This is a fixed, 4-tab bottom navigation bar with a center floating action ("scan") button — not a generic repeatable item list. The 4 tabs (Home, Card, Services, Menu) are hardcoded compositions (fixed icon + fixed label each), not a `tabs[]` array, because no two tabs share an interchangeable shape in the evidence — each has its own icon and its own always-visible label text.

### Sub-components

None exposed as a configurable API surface — every tab's icon and label is fixed (not swappable) in the extracted evidence, and the center scan button's icon identity could not be resolved (see Known gaps). See Structure for the full icon-to-tab reference mapping.

### Configuration examples

**Example 1 — Home tab active (default)**

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `activeTab` | home | – |

**Example 2 — Card tab active**

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `activeTab` | card | – |

**Example 3 — Services tab active**

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `activeTab` | services | – |

**Example 4 — Menu tab active**

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `activeTab` | menu | – |

## Structure

### Anatomy

```
Navigation (root, VERTICAL auto-layout, 343 × 96)
└─ wrapper frame (name varies by variant — see Known gaps; HORIZONTAL, space-between)
   ├─ Home tab   — icon: "house" (INSTANCE, 24×24)         + label "Home"     (TEXT, 12px)
   ├─ Card tab   — icon: "credit-card" (INSTANCE, 24×24)   + label "Card"     (TEXT, 12px)
   ├─ Scan button — pill (56×56, cornerRadius 999)
   │  ├─ icon frame (32×32, raw VECTOR × 5 paths — identity unresolved)
   │  └─ "Status Indicator" (RECTANGLE, 41×8 — no fill/stroke captured)
   ├─ Services tab — icon: "layout-grid" (INSTANCE, 24×24) + label "Services" (TEXT, 12px)
   └─ Menu tab   — icon: "settings-2" (INSTANCE, 24×24)    + label "Menu"     (TEXT, 12px)
```

### Root container

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| width | 343 | Identical across all 4 `activeTab` variants. |
| height | 96 | |
| padding (top, bottom, start, end) | space-24 (24), space-40 (40), space-24 (24), space-24 (24) | Bottom padding (40) is generous — likely reserves room for a device home-indicator bar below the tab row. |
| layoutMode | VERTICAL | |
| wrapper frame name | "Home Container" / "Container" / "Menu option" / "Menu Item" (varies by `activeTab`) | Authoring inconsistency — geometry is identical regardless of name. See Known gaps. |

### Tab row (Home, Card, Scan, Services, Menu — left to right)

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| tab item size (w × h) | 47 × 44 | Each of the 4 tab items (icon + label, vertical stack). |
| tab icon size | 24 × 24 | Lucide instances: `house` (Home), `credit-card` (Card), `layout-grid` (Services), `settings-2` (Menu). |
| tab label fontSize | 12 | |
| scan button pill | 56 × 68 outer, 56 × 56 inner pill (cornerRadius 999) | Contains one 32×32 icon frame (raw vector, identity unresolved) and a 41×8 "Status Indicator" rectangle with no resolved fill/stroke (purpose unclear — see Known gaps). |
| item order | Home, Card, Scan, Services, Menu | Scan button sits in the visual center (3rd of 5), splitting the 4 tabs 2-and-2. |

## Color

All tokens below are measured from `colorWalk[]`.

### Tab state (active vs inactive)

| Element | active tab (icon + label) | inactive tabs (icon + label) |
|---|---|---|
| Tab icon stroke / Tab label text | Content/Primary `#0f172a` | Content/Neutral `#a3a3a3` |
| Scan button icon stroke | Content/White `#ffffff` | Content/White `#ffffff` |
| Scan button pill fill | unresolved — no fill captured | unresolved — no fill captured |
| Root background | Background/White `#ffffff` | Background/White `#ffffff` |
| Root top border | Border/Primary `#dde8fb` — only on the `home` variant; see Known gaps | Background/Neutral `#e5e5e5` |

> Only `activeTab=home` measures a colored top border; `card`/`services`/`menu` all measure a plain gray border instead of a matching highlight. Measured fact, flagged in Known gaps — not resolved here.

### Variable collection

The root background/border tokens are bound via the **"Colors semantic"** variable collection (`Light` / `Dark` modes). Light/Dark theming is handled automatically by these semantic tokens; it is not a component-level property.

---

## Voice / Screen reader

> `_base.json` carries no dedicated accessibility annotations for this component (no aria layer names, no accessible-name overrides). The states below follow standard bottom-navigation accessibility conventions given the measured `activeTab` evidence — treat as a starting point, not a Figma-sourced fact, and verify with an accessibility reviewer (see Known gaps).

### Focus order

| # | Layer | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Home tab | |
| 2 | Card tab | |
| 3 | Scan button (center action) | |
| 4 | Services tab | |
| 5 | Menu tab | |

### Per-state guidance

**tab: inactive**

| Platform | Behavior |
|---|---|
| VoiceOver | Role: Tab. Label: `<tab label>, tab`. Traits: Button. Hint: "Double-tap to switch to this section." |
| TalkBack | Role: Tab. Label: `<tab label>`. State: Not selected. |
| ARIA | `role="tab"`; `aria-selected="false"`; part of a tablist/navigation role container. |

**tab: active (`activeTab`)**

| Platform | Behavior |
|---|---|
| VoiceOver | Label: `<tab label>, selected, tab`. Traits: Button, Selected. No activation hint — already showing this section. |
| TalkBack | Label: `<tab label>, selected`. State: Selected. |
| ARIA | `aria-selected="true"`. |

**scan button**

| Platform | Behavior |
|---|---|
| VoiceOver | Role: Button. Label: accessible name inferred — no text label exists on this control (see Known gaps). Hint: "Double-tap to activate." |
| TalkBack | Role: Button. Label: "Scan" (placeholder). State: Clickable. |
| ARIA | Requires an explicit `aria-label` since this control has no visible text; the icon's identity could not be resolved from the extraction — confirm the intended action name with design before shipping copy. |

---

## Cross-references

- **API ↔ Structure:** `activeTab` (API) selects which Tab-row entry is highlighted; the icon-to-tab mapping (house/credit-card/layout-grid/settings-2) lives in Structure since no configurable property exposes it.
- **API ↔ Color:** `activeTab` (API) selects the "active tab" vs "inactive tabs" column in the Color section.
- **API ↔ Voice:** `activeTab` drives the `aria-selected` / "selected" state documented per-tab in Voice.
- **Structure ↔ Known gaps:** the wrapper-name inconsistency and the two unresolved scan-button elements (icon identity, pill fill, Status Indicator) are documented once in Structure and cross-flagged in Known gaps rather than repeated.
